title: Pico de Gallo (Fresh Tomato Salsa)

ingredients.name: ripe tomatoes, seeds removed, finely diced

subs.name: ripe mango for a sweet, fruity salsa that pairs well with fish

ingredients.name: green chilli (jalapeño or serrano), finely chopped

subs.name: finely diced capsicum for a milder version suitable for kids
a small splash of apple cider vinegar adds similar brightness

steps.text: Halve the tomatoes and use a teaspoon to scoop out the seeds and watery pulp — this keeps the salsa from becoming soupy. Dice the flesh into small, even pieces about 5 mm square.
Combine the diced tomato, onion, green chilli and coriander in a bowl. Season generously with salt and add the lime juice.

Toss everything together and taste — the salsa should be bright, a little salty and lively with citrus. Add more lime or chilli to taste. Let it rest for 5 minutes before serving so the flavours can meld.
Serve immediately or within a few hours. After 2–3 hours refrigerated, the tomato starts to release liquid and the salsa loses its crunch — make it fresh whenever possible.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: tomato → ripe mango for a sweet, fruity salsa that pairs well with fish; green-chilli → finely diced capsicum for a milder version suitable for kids; lemon → a small splash of apple cider vinegar adds similar brightness
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This is essentially a Mexican version of the Indian kachumber salad — dice everything small and serve alongside any grilled meat or dal-rice meal for a fresh counterpoint. Green chillies available in any Indian vegetable market are the right substitute for jalapeño. Nimbu juice is ideal here.
